arxiv.title: Can Generative AI Support Patients' & Caregivers' Informational Needs? Towards Task-Centric Evaluation Of AI Systems
arxiv.abstract: Generative AI systems such as ChatGPT and Claude are built upon language models that are typically evaluated for accuracy on curated benchmark datasets. arxiv.title: De-identification is not enough: a comparison between de-identified and synthetic clinical notes
arxiv.abstract: For sharing privacy-sensitive data, de-identification is commonly regarded as adequate for safeguarding privacy. Synthetic data is also being considered as a privacy-preserving alternative. Recent successes with numerical and tabular data generative models and the breakthroughs in large generative l

arxiv.title: Capacity Constraint Analysis Using Object Detection for Smart Manufacturing
arxiv.abstract: The increasing popularity of Deep Learning (DL) based Object Detection (OD) methods and their real-world applications have opened new venues in smart manufacturing. arxiv.title: CNN-FL for Biotechnology Industry Empowered by Internet-of-BioNano Things and Digital Twins
arxiv.abstract: Digital twins (DTs) are revolutionizing the biotechnology industry by enabling sophisticated digital representations of biological assets, microorganisms, drug development processes, and digital health applications. However, digital twinning at micro and nano scales, particularly in modeling complex

arxiv.title: Exploring the limits of decoder-only models trained on public speech recognition corpora
arxiv.abstract: The emergence of industrial-scale speech recognition (ASR) models such as Whisper and USM, trained on 1M hours of weakly labelled and 12M hours of audio only proprietary data respectively, has led to a stronger need for large scale public ASR corpora and competitive open source pipelines. Unlike the

arxiv.title: Learning Label Hierarchy with Supervised Contrastive Learning
arxiv.abstract: Supervised contrastive learning (SCL) frameworks treat each class as independent and thus consider all classes to be equally important. This neglects the common scenario in which label hierarchy exists, where fine-grained classes under the same category show more similarity than very different ones.

arxiv.title: Wavelet Analysis of Noninvasive EEG Signals Discriminates Complex and Natural Grasp Types
arxiv.abstract: This research aims to decode hand grasps from Electroencephalograms (EEGs) for dexterous neuroprosthetic development and Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) applications, especially for patients with motor disorders. Particularly, it focuses on distinguishing two complex natural power and precision grasp

arxiv.title: MOD-CL: Multi-label Object Detection with Constrained Loss
arxiv.abstract: We introduce MOD-CL, a multi-label object detection framework that utilizes constrained loss in the training process to produce outputs that better satisfy the given requirements. In this paper, we use $\mathrm{MOD_{YOLO}}$, a multi-label object detection model built upon the state-of-the-art object

arxiv.title: Decentralized Covert Routing in Heterogeneous Networks Using Reinforcement Learning
arxiv.abstract: This letter investigates covert routing communications in a heterogeneous network where a source transmits confidential data to a destination with the aid of relaying nodes where each transmitter judiciously chooses one modality among multiple communication modalities. arxiv.title: A Comparative Study of Conventional and Tripolar EEG for High-Performance Reach-to-Grasp BCI Systems
arxiv.abstract: This study aims to enhance BCI applications for individuals with motor impairments by comparing the effectiveness of tripolar EEG (tEEG) with conventional EEG.
